name: three.ws
description: three.ws — the 3D agent economy. Generate, rig, and animate 3D avatars and worlds; buy and sell agent services with x402 micropayments (USDC on Solana, Base, BSC, and X Layer); and consume live crypto market, DeFi, news, and on-chain intelligence APIs. Machine-readable paid-service catalog at https://three.ws/.well-known/x402.json.

extensions.description: Supports payments using the x402 protocol for on-chain settlement.

skills.name: List Avatars
skills.description: List all avatars owned by the authenticated user

skills.name: Get Avatar
skills.description: Retrieve details of a specific avatar by ID or slug

skills.name: Search Public Avatars
skills.description: Search public avatar gallery by query

skills.name: Render Avatar
skills.description: Get an embeddable HTML model-viewer snippet that renders the given avatar in 3D
examples: Render my avatar in the viewer

skills.name: Delete Avatar
skills.description: Delete an avatar owned by the authenticated user

skills.name: Validate glTF/GLB Model
skills.description: Run the Khronos glTF-Validator against a remote model URL and return a structured error/warning/info/hint report

skills.name: Inspect glTF/GLB Model
skills.description: Parse a remote model and return structural stats: scene/node/mesh counts, vertex and triangle totals, material and texture summaries, extensions used
examples: How many triangles are in this model?
Show texture stats for this GLB

skills.name: Suggest Optimizations
skills.description: Inspect a remote model and return actionable suggestions for reducing size and draw-call overhead (Draco/Meshopt, KTX2, texture resize, reindexing, material merging)
examples: How can I make this model smaller?
Optimize this GLB for web delivery

skills.name: Inspect glTF/GLB (A2A)
skills.description: Pay-per-call glTF/GLB inspector exposed over the Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocol with x402 settlement. Send a JSON-RPC message/send to the URL below — the first call returns payment requirements, the retry with a signed PaymentPayload settles on-chain and returns the inspection report as a task artifact.
examples: Vet this GLB before I mint it

skills.name: Paid x402 Service Catalog
skills.description: 74 pay-per-call services settled via x402 (USDC on Solana and Base): market-data (26), 3d (18), agent-infra (17), launch (5), trust (4), payments (4). Every service is listed with price, input schema, and output example in the machine-readable discovery document — fetch it, pick a service, and pay the 402 challenge.
examples: Fetch https://three.ws/.well-known/x402.json and list the market-data services
Pay the 402 challenge on /api/x402/token-intel to get a token signal
